description: R Hearst Plan to Rebuild Cheers France Says Hoggson FOOD AND SHELTER MUST OF PROVIDED NOW SO LABOR CAN AID INDUSTRY AT WARS END Four Great Cities Still Occupied by Ger many or Under Fire 29594 Buildings Damaged and More Than Half the Structures in 148 Towns Demolished GOVERNMENT COMMITTEE REPORTS GREAT TASK ALREADY UNDER WAY Asa member of the American Industrial Commission which visited France a little more than a year ago Mr Hoggson was offered an extraordi nary opportunity to view the terrible destruction wrought by the German guns and to carefully study the needs of reconstruction and rehabilitation in the invaded districts It is therefore from a practical firsthand knowledge that he views the problem and appreciates the significance of Ammicas rebuilding the homes for the French people while the French devote their attention to reestab lishing their industries By Noble Foster Hoggson President of Hoggson Brothers and a Member of the FranceRebuilding Committee of This City THE proposed plan suggested by the Hearst papersthat each of our American cities undertake as a labor of love the rebuilding of one ruined Blench town or villageshould meet with instant enthusi asm and favor the American people If such
